textPlain: Nerds do tend to forget that people can make procedural errors.

we have encountered a fatal technical problem that prevents us from concluding the election and accessing the final tally,How is someone losing their key a "technical problem"?we will adopt a 2-out-of-3 threshold mechanism for the management of private keysThe trustee responsible has resigned so why weaken security going forward?

It sounds like the technical problem is that they spent more time thinking about cryptography itself than they did about the prudent application of it.Confidentiality that undermines availability might be good cryptography but it violates the basic tenets of information security.
